pi_items.source: Cannot verify without access to Fiji/ImageJ runtime. The Fluorescent Cells sample is a built-in ImageJ/Fiji sample, but the actual number of particles depends on threshold settings and image analysis parameters.

ImageJ ResultsTable source code (https://imagej.net/ij/source/ij/measure/ResultsTable.java) shows standard column names. The column name for mean gray value is 'Mean' (not 'Mean_Gray_Value'). The task description (line 6) states 'The resulting CSV must contain the Mean column' (singular). The export_result.sh script checks for any column containing 'Mean' but does not verify a separate 'Mean_Gray_Value' column exists.

File path matches setup_task.sh (line 9: RESULTS_DIR='$DATA_DIR/results') and export_result.sh (line 12: RESULT_FILE='/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/vesicle_distribution.csv'). Standard ImageJ_Data directory structure confirmed in setup_imagej.sh.

Confirmed via https://bioimagebook.github.io/chapters/1-concepts/4-colors/imagej.html that 'Fluorescent Cells (400K)' is accessed via File › Open Samples › Fluorescent Cells (400K) in ImageJ. This is a standard multichannel composite image sample with red and green channels.

pi_items.verified_value: Multichannel composite RGB image with red and green channels
pi_items.source: Referenced in multiple ImageJ documentation pages (https://bioimagebook.github.io/chapters/1-concepts/4-colors/imagej.html, https://petebankhead.gitbooks.io/imagej-intro/content/chapters/colors/colors.html) as a composite image used for teaching multichannel image analysis.

privileged_info_summary: The intracellular_distribution_edm task uses the built-in 'Fluorescent Cells (400K)' sample from ImageJ/Fiji, a real (non-synthetic) multichannel composite image with red and green channels. The task outputs results to /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/vesicle_distribution.csv as specified. However, the metadata claims that the required columns are ['Mean', 'Mean_Gray_Value'], but ImageJ's standard measurement column name is only 'Mean' (confirmed from the ImageJ ResultsTable source code). The task description itself only mentions the 'Mean' column as required. The particle counts (30-200) and distance ranges (2.0-100.0 pixels) cannot be verified without running the actual analysis in Fiji on the sample image, as these depend on thresholding and analysis parameter choices.
